title: Event
description: A structured event extracted from a source document. Events are the atomic unit of signal in Unicorn Protocol — they represent something that happened to or within a company, grounded in evidence from the source.

event_type.description: The category of event. Must be one of the defined event taxonomy types.

company_id.description: Resolved company identifier. Null when the company has not yet been matched to a canonical entity in the system.

company_hint.description: Hints for company resolution when company_id is not yet resolved. Used by the matching pipeline to link this event to a canonical company entity.

name.description: Primary company name as it appears in the source.

domain.description: Company website domain if mentioned or inferable from the source.

aliases.description: Alternative names, abbreviations, or former names for the company found in the source.

title.description: Short human-readable summary of the event (e.g. "Acme Corp raises $50M Series B").

description.description: Longer detail about the event, providing context beyond the title.

occurred_at.description: When the event occurred, in ISO 8601 format. May be a full datetime (2024-03-15T10:30:00Z) or a date (2024-03-15) depending on available precision.

occurred_at_precision.description: How precise the occurred_at date actually is. Sources often say 'in Q3 2024' or 'last year' — this field captures that the specific date is an approximation.

source_id.description: Identifier of the source document this event was extracted from.

evidence.description: Supporting evidence for this event. Every event must have at least one piece of evidence grounding it in the source text.

confidence.description: Model's confidence that this event actually occurred as described, from 0.0 (no confidence) to 1.0 (certain).

confidence_rationale.description: Explanation of why the model assigned this confidence level. Should reference the evidence strength and any ambiguities.

metadata.description: Freeform object for event-type-specific fields (e.g. funding_amount, role_title, acquirer_name, headcount_affected). Structure varies by event_type.

extraction_version.description: Schema version tag indicating which version of the extraction schema produced this event.
